facts_disclosed: Khao man gai vendor, buys rice from Auntie Somjit at Khlong Toei market — 38 baht per kilo, 15 kilos daily

turns.content: hiii can you help me figure out something?? I need to calculate how much rice I use per month for my cart. I know how much per day but my brain cannot do the monthly number haha
Sure, I can help with that! How much rice do you use per day, and how many days per month do you typically work?

okay so I buy 15 kilos every morning from my rice lady. Auntie Somjit at Khlong Toei market. she always have it ready for me when I get there 4:30am na ka. very reliable. 38 baht per kilo so the rice alone already cost me a lot!!
Alright, so 15 kilos a day at 38 baht per kilo — that's 570 baht per day just for rice. How many days a week do you run your cart? That'll help us get the monthly total.

I work 6 days na ka. Monday to Saturday. Sunday I rest. well actually I still go to market on Sunday sometimes haha but I don't open the cart. so 6 days per week. I sell khao man gai — you know chicken rice?? that is my thing

Khao man gai — great dish! Okay so 6 days a week, let's say roughly 26 working days per month. That gives us:

- **Rice per month:** 15 kg x 26 days = **390 kilos**
- **Rice cost per month:** 390 kg x 38 baht = **14,820 baht**

So you're spending just under 15,000 baht a month on rice alone. Does that sound about right to you?
arai wa!! 14,820 baht just rice!! I know it was a lot but seeing the number like that is scary haha. and that's not even the chicken. chicken is the big cost. rice is number 2

Yeah, it adds up fast when you look at the monthly number! But that's the reality of running a food business — ingredients are the biggest expense. If you want, I can help you calculate total ingredient costs if you tell me roughly what you spend on chicken and other supplies per day too. That way you can see the full picture.

okay let me think. chicken I buy maybe 40 kilos per day. the good chicken na ka, fresh from the market. about 85 baht per kilo. then ginger, garlic, cucumber, sauce ingredient... maybe 800 baht per day for everything else. oh and the charcoal!! 200 baht per day for charcoal

Let me break it all down for you based on 26 days per month:

| Item | Daily | Monthly |
|------|-------|---------|
| Rice (15 kg x 38 ฿) | 570 ฿ | 14,820 ฿ |
| Chicken (40 kg x 85 ฿) | 3,400 ฿ | 88,400 ฿ |
| Other ingredients | 800 ฿ | 20,800 ฿ |
| Charcoal | 200 ฿ | 5,200 ฿ |
| **Total** | **4,970 ฿** | **129,220 ฿** |

So roughly **129,000 baht per month** on ingredients and charcoal. Chicken is definitely the big one at almost 88,400 baht. How does that compare to what you're bringing in from sales?

wow thank you na ka this is so clear!! okay so I sell about 200 plates per day. each plate 50 baht regular size, some people order big size 70 baht. let's say average 55 baht per plate. so 200 x 55 = 11,000 baht per day right??

You got it! 200 plates at an average of 55 baht = 11,000 baht per day. So monthly:

- **Revenue:** 11,000 x 26 = **286,000 baht**
- **Ingredient costs:** 129,220 baht
- **Gross profit:** roughly **156,780 baht** before other expenses like rent for your spot, gas for transport, etc.

That's a healthy margin — about 55%. You're making good money from those 200 plates! Is the rice from Auntie Somjit good quality? Because at 38 baht per kilo, that's not the cheapest, so the quality better be worth it.

yes!! Auntie Somjit rice is very good na ka. jasmine rice, long grain. she know exactly what I need for khao man gai. the rice must be right because I cook it in chicken fat and chicken broth, so if the rice is bad quality everything is bad. I try cheaper rice before and the texture was wrong. customers notice!! for khao man gai the rice is the star not just the chicken

That makes total sense — for khao man gai the rice has to absorb all that chicken fat and broth flavour perfectly, so you need grains that hold up and stay fluffy without getting mushy. Cheap rice would fall apart or get too sticky. Your customers can tell the difference because they eat it every day.

So it sounds like the 38 baht per kilo is money well spent. Auntie Somjit sounds like a great supplier — having someone reliable who has your 15 kilos ready at 4:30am every morning is worth a lot.

Want me to help you track these numbers in a spreadsheet or something? It might help you see trends over time, especially if ingredient prices change.

ohhh spreadsheet is good idea!! but I am not good with computer haha. I do everything on my phone. can you help me set up something simple? like just daily number I can type in fast before I go to bed

Absolutely! Here's a super simple system you can do on your phone — you can use Google Sheets (free app) and make one sheet with just these columns:

1. **Date**
2. **Plates sold** (just the total number)
3. **Revenue** (total baht collected)
4. **Rice cost** (570 if you buy your usual 15 kg)
5. **Chicken cost** (however much you spent)
6. **Other costs** (everything else)

That's only 6 things to type each night, takes maybe 2 minutes. Google Sheets will auto-calculate totals at the bottom.

Want me to write out the formulas you'd need, or keep it even simpler than that? The key is making it fast enough that you actually do it every day after a long day at the cart.

yes keep it simple na ka!! I wake up 4am and by the time I finish cleaning the cart at night I am so tired haha. okay I will try Google Sheets. thank you so much!! this really help me see where my money going. 129,000 baht per month on ingredient alone!! I need to keep track of this properly 🙏

You're welcome! And honestly, the fact that you're thinking about tracking your costs means you're already ahead of a lot of food vendors. Here's the quick setup:

1. Download Google Sheets app
2. Make a new sheet, type those 6 column headers in row 1
3. Each night just fill in one row — takes 1-2 minutes
4. At the end of the month, highlight the number columns and it'll show you the sum at the bottom

Start simple, don't overthink it. You can always add more detail later. And with those 4:30am mornings, sleep is more important than a perfect spreadsheet! Good luck with the cart — 200 plates a day of khao man gai is serious work. 🙏
